channel.title: Posts on Ishwara's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Ishwara's Blog

item.title: How I've run major projects
item.description:
From experience running large projects, I’ve developed some personal frameworks that have helped me ship better work.

Start with the outcome

Before anything else, be clear about what done looks like. A fuzzy goal produces fuzzy results.

Work backwards

Once you know the outcome, reverse-engineer the milestones. What must be true one week before launch? One month before?

The weekly review

Every week, ask: what did I ship, what’s blocked, and what do I need to decide?

item.title: Staring into the abyss as a core life skill
item.description: There’s a kind of discomfort that comes from sitting with hard problems. Most people flee it. The ones who don’t — they tend to do interesting things.

item.title: Searching for outliers
item.description: The best work comes from people who have internalized high standards so deeply that mediocrity feels physically uncomfortable.

item.title: Get real, get curious
item.description: Curiosity is underrated as a career strategy. The people who win long-term aren’t usually the most disciplined — they’re the ones who can’t stop thinking about the problem.

item.title: In defense of case studies
item.description: Reading case studies is one of the most efficient ways to compress decades of experience into weeks. The key is not just reading — it’s actively asking why at every decision point.
